qtype: Ratio division, fraction simplification, percentage conversion, percentage reduction, reverse percentage, simple interest.
tex:
(a) (i) (b) Divide \$24 in the ratio $7 : 5$.
\newline

(c) Write \$24.60 as a fraction of \$2870.
\newline

Give your answer in its lowest terms.
\newline

(d) Write \$1.92 as a percentage of \$1.60 .
\newline

(e) In a sale the original prices are reduced by 15\%.
\newline

(i) (f) Calculate the sale price of a book that has an original price of \$12.
\newline

(g) Calculate the original price of a jacket that has a sale price of \$38.25 .
\newline

(h) (i) (i) Dean invests \$500 for 10 years at a rate of 1.7\% per year simple interest.
\newline

Calculate the total interest earned during the 10 years.
\newline

(j) Ollie invests \$200 at a rate of 0.0035\% per day compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the value of Ollie's investment at the end of 1 year.
\newline

[1 year = 365 days.
\newline
]

(k) Edna invests \$500 at a rate of $r$\% per year compound interest.
\newline

At the end of 6 years, the value of Edna's investment is \$559.78 .
\newline

Find the value of $r$.
\newline

tex:

\newline

\newline

\newline

Find the position vector of $M$.
\newline

tex:
The speed, $v$ km/h, of each of 200 cars passing a building is measured.
\newline

The table shows the results.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Speed ($v$ km/h) & $0 < v \leqslant 20$ & $20 < v \leqslant 40$ & $40 < v \leqslant 45$ & $45 < v \leqslant 50$ & $50 < v \leqslant 60$ & $60 < v \leqslant 80$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 16 & 34 & 62 & 58 & 26 & 4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) Calculate an estimate of the mean.
\newline

(b) (i) (c) Use the frequency table to complete the cumulative frequency table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Speed ($v$ km/h) & $v \leqslant 20$ & $v \leqslant 40$ & $v \leqslant 45$ & $v \leqslant 50$ & $v \leqslant 60$ & $v \leqslant 80$ \\
\hline
Cumulative frequency & 16 & 50 &  &  & 196 & 200 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(d) On the grid, draw a cumulative frequency diagram.
\newline

(e) Use your diagram to find an estimate of

(a) the upper quartile,

(b) the number of cars with a speed greater than 35 km/h.
\newline

(f) Two of the 200 cars are chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that they both have a speed greater than 50 km/h.
\newline

(g) A new frequency table is made by combining intervals.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|}
\hline
Speed ($v$ km/h) & $0 < v \leqslant 40$ & $40 < v \leqslant 50$ & $50 < v \leqslant 80$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 50 & 120 & 30 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
On the grid, draw a histogram to show the information in this table.
\newline

content.content:
Calculate an estimate of the mean.
\newline

Use the frequency table to complete the cumulative frequency table.
\newline

On the grid, draw a cumulative frequency diagram.
\newline

Use your diagram to find an estimate of

(a) the upper quartile,

\AnswerLine[][km/h]
\Marks{1}

(b) the number of cars with a speed greater than 35 km/h.
\newline

Two of the 200 cars are chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that they both have a speed greater than 50 km/h.
\newline

A new frequency table is made by combining intervals.
\newline

On the grid, draw a histogram to show the information in this table.
\newline

\newline
The table shows the results.
\newline

tex:
The diagram shows a field $ABCD$.
\newline

The bearing of $B$ from $A$ is $140^{\circ}$.
\newline

$C$ is due east of $B$ and $D$ is due north of $C$.
\newline

$AB = 400$ m, $BC = 350$ m and $CD = 450$ m.
\newline

(a) Find the bearing of $D$ from $B$.
\newline

(b) Calculate the distance from $D$ to $A$.
\newline

(c) Jono runs around the field from $A$ to $B$, $B$ to $C$, $C$ to $D$ and $D$ to $A$.
\newline

He runs at a speed of 3 m/s.
\newline

Calculate the total time Jono takes to run around the field.
\newline

Give your answer in minutes and seconds, correct to the nearest second.
\newline

content.content:
Find the bearing of $D$ from $B$.
\newline

Calculate the distance from $D$ to $A$.
\newline

Jono runs around the field from $A$ to $B$, $B$ to $C$, $C$ to $D$ and $D$ to $A$.
\newline
He runs at a speed of 3 m/s.
\newline

Calculate the total time Jono takes to run around the field.
\newline
Give your answer in minutes and seconds, correct to the nearest second.
\newline

stem.content:
The diagram shows a field $ABCD$.
\newline
The bearing of $B$ from $A$ is $140^{\circ}$.
\newline
$C$ is due east of $B$ and $D$ is due north of $C$.
\newline
$AB = 400$ m, $BC = 350$ m and $CD = 450$ m.
\newline

_rawBody:
\relinput{Figures/Q05-FieldBearing.tex}

The diagram shows a field $ABCD$.
\newline
The bearing of $B$ from $A$ is $140^{\circ}$.
\newline
$C$ is due east of $B$ and $D$ is due north of $C$.
\newline
$AB = 400$ m, $BC = 350$ m and $CD = 450$ m.

\begin{parts}
\item Find the bearing of $D$ from $B$.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item Calculate the distance from $D$ to $A$.

\AnswerLine[][m]
\Marks{6}

\item Jono runs around the field from $A$ to $B$, $B$ to $C$, $C$ to $D$ and $D$ to $A$.
\newline
He runs at a speed of 3 m/s.

Calculate the total time Jono takes to run around the field.
\newline
Give your answer in minutes and seconds, correct to the nearest second.

\AnswerLine[][min ...................................... s]
\Marks{4}
\end{parts}

qtype: function evaluation, composite functions, inverse functions, algebraic fractions with functions
tex:
\[
f(x) = 3x + 2 \qquad g(x) = x^2 + 1 \qquad h(x) = 4^x
\]

(a) Find $h(4)$.
\newline

(b) Find $fg(1)$.
\newline

(c) Find $gf(x)$ in the form $ax^2 + bx + c$.
\newline

(d) Find $x$ when $f(x) = g(7)$.
\newline

(e) Find $f^{-1}(x)$.
\newline

(f) Find $\dfrac{g(x)}{f(x)} + x$.
\newline

Give your answer as a single fraction, in terms of $x$, in its simplest form.
\newline

(g) Find $x$ when $h^{-1}(x) = 2$.
\newline

content.content:
Find $h(4)$.
\newline

Find $fg(1)$.
\newline

Find $gf(x)$ in the form $ax^2 + bx + c$.
\newline

Find $x$ when $f(x) = g(7)$.
\newline

Find $f^{-1}(x)$.
\newline

Find $\dfrac{g(x)}{f(x)} + x$.
\newline
Give your answer as a single fraction, in terms of $x$, in its simplest form.
\newline

Find $x$ when $h^{-1}(x) = 2$.
\newline

tex:
Tanya plants some seeds.
\newline

The probability that a seed will produce flowers is 0.8 .
\newline

When a seed produces flowers, the probability that the flowers are red is 0.6 and the probability that the flowers are yellow is 0.3 .
\newline

(a) Tanya has a seed that produces flowers.
\newline

Find the probability that the flowers are not red and not yellow.
\newline

(b) (i) (c) Complete the tree diagram.
\newline

(d) Find the probability that a seed chosen at random produces red flowers.
\newline

(e) Tanya chooses a seed at random.
\newline

Find the probability that this seed does not produce red flowers and does not produce yellow flowers.
\newline

(f) Two of the seeds are chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that one produces flowers and one does not produce flowers.
\newline

content.content:

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

Tanya chooses a seed at random.
\newline

\newline

Two of the seeds are chosen at random.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

tex:
(a) 

Triangle $ABC$ is mathematically similar to triangle $PQR$.
\newline

The area of triangle $ABC$ is 16 cm$^2$.
\newline

(i) (b) Calculate the area of triangle $PQR$.
\newline

(c) The triangles are the cross-sections of prisms which are also mathematically similar.
\newline

The volume of the smaller prism is 320 cm$^3$.
\newline

Calculate the length of the larger prism.
\newline

(d) A cylinder with radius 6 cm and height $h$ cm has the same volume as a sphere with radius 4.5 cm.
\newline

Find the value of $h$.
\newline

[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.
\newline
]

(e) A solid metal cube of side 20 cm is melted down and made into 40 solid spheres, each of radius $r$ cm.
\newline

Find the value of $r$.
\newline

[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.
\newline
]

(f) A solid cylinder has radius $x$ cm and height $\frac{7x}{2}$ cm.
\newline

The surface area of a sphere with radius $R$ cm is equal to the total surface area of the cylinder.
\newline

Find an expression for $R$ in terms of $x$.
\newline

[The surface area, $A$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $A = 4\pi r^2$.
\newline
]

\newline

\newline

\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item 
\relinput{Figures/Q08-SimilarTriangles.tex}

Triangle $ABC$ is mathematically similar to triangle $PQR$.
\newline
The area of triangle $ABC$ is 16 cm$^2$.

\begin{subparts}
\item Calculate the area of triangle $PQR$.

\AnswerLine[][cm$^2$]
\Marks{2}

\item The triangles are the cross-sections of prisms which are also mathematically similar.
\newline
The volume of the smaller prism is 320 cm$^3$.

Calculate the length of the larger prism.

\AnswerLine[][cm]
\Marks{3}
\end{subparts}

\item A cylinder with radius 6 cm and height $h$ cm has the same volume as a sphere with radius 4.5 cm.

Find the value of $h$.
\newline
[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.]

\AnswerLine[$h = $][]
\Marks{3}

\item A solid metal cube of side 20 cm is melted down and made into 40 solid spheres, each of radius $r$ cm.

Find the value of $r$.
\newline
[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.]

\AnswerLine[$r = $][]
\Marks{3}

\item A solid cylinder has radius $x$ cm and height $\frac{7x}{2}$ cm.
\newline
The surface area of a sphere with radius $R$ cm is equal to the total surface area of the cylinder.

Find an expression for $R$ in terms of $x$.
\newline
[The surface area, $A$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $A = 4\pi r^2$.]

\AnswerLine[$R = $][]
\Marks{3}
\end{parts}

qtype: completing the square, solving quadratic equations, sketching graphs, intersection points
tex:
(a) (i) (b) Write $x^2 + 8x - 9$ in the form $(x + k)^2 + h$.
\newline

(c) Use your answer to part (a)(i) to solve the equation $x^2 + 8x - 9 = 0$.
\newline

(d) The solutions of the equation $x^2 + bx + c = 0$ are $\frac{-7 + \sqrt{61}}{2}$ and $\frac{-7 - \sqrt{61}}{2}$.
\newline

Find the value of $b$ and the value of $c$.
\newline

(e) (i) (f) On the diagram,
(g) sketch the graph of $y = (x - 1)^2$, [2]

(h) sketch the graph of $y = \frac{1}{2}x + 1$.
\newline
 [2]

(ii) The graphs of $y = (x - 1)^2$ and $y = \frac{1}{2}x + 1$ intersect at $A$ and $B$.
\newline

Find the length of $AB$.
\newline

tex:
(a) $y = x^4 - 4x^3$
(i) (b) Find the value of $y$ when $x = -1$.
\newline

(c) Find the two stationary points on the graph of $y = x^4 - 4x^3$.
\newline

(d) $y = x^p + 2x^q$

$\frac{dy}{dx} = 11x^{10} + 10x^4$, where $\frac{dy}{dx}$ is the derived function.
\newline

Find the value of $p$ and the value of $q$.
\newline
